hero_subtitle: SFTY provides regulators a sovereign, auditable operating layer built for phased deployment, role-based access, and seamless integration with existing government systems.

problem_title: Government cloud projects face complex deployment risks
problem_description: Procurement and security teams encounter challenges supporting data sovereignty, controlled access and audit log integrity in multi-provider supervisory environments. Without clear deployment frameworks, government cloud initiatives risk slow implementation and uncertain operational governance.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY organizes sovereign infrastructure with role-based access, audit logs, evidence-grade tracking and phased implementation frameworks to help regulators deploy multi-provider supervisory environments confidently and efficiently.
workflow_title: From deployment controls to audit logs
workflow_description: SFTY structures government cloud deployment workflows by linking access policies, role permissions, audit trails and evidence governance into secure supervisory records.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another cloud. Sovereign infrastructure ready
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides a secure, auditable supervisory layer designed for sovereign environments, connecting fragmented data to support government procurement, phased deployment and governance needs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated authorities requiring secure government cloud infrastructure with controlled access, audit-grade evidence logging and procurement-ready phased implementation from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for government regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators a secure supervisory layer connecting VASP data, licensing, wallets, transactions, cases and reports into one evidence-ready platform for market-level digital asset oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support sovereign cloud deployment?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports sovereign cloud by enabling controlled, phased deployment with role-based access, audit logs, data segregation and integration inside secure government environments.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and evidence-grade audit trails to support structured, defensible supervisory records ready for review and reporting.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with government registries, reporting workflows and multiple provider outputs to streamline supervision within existing public-sector infrastructure.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics or tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a supervisory layer that connects and structures data from multiple analytics and compliance tools without replacing underlying providers.
final_title: Review secure deployment for government cloud
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports sovereign infrastructure with role-based access, audit logs, phased rollout and integration readiness.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
